info.name: Carlton Hotel API
info.description:
Full API surface through P10 (Foundation → Notifications/Chat → Ops Dashboard). Companion to backend/docs/API_GUIDE_{MOBILE,DASHBOARD,WEBSITE}.md.

Import carlton-api.postman_environment.json alongside this collection and select it as the active environment — it's pre-populated with a fresh super_admin token, a live Ahmad (checked-in guest) token, and every uuid this collection references, all captured from a freshly-seeded database (`php artisan migrate:fresh --seed`).

Tokens go stale whenever the backend is re-seeded. Re-run '02 - Auth > Login as Super Admin' (and the other Login requests as needed) to refresh — each has a test script that writes the new token straight back into the environment.

Requests marked ⚠️ or ❌ are intentionally destructive or intentionally-failing demonstrations — read their description before running.

request.description: Super admin bypasses every permission check, so {{staff_token}} (used by default across this whole collection) can call any admin endpoint immediately. Run this first if the pre-populated environment token has gone stale.
reception preset: reservations.view/create/cancel, folios.view/settle, service_requests.view.
events preset: service_requests.view, tickets.view/assign/respond. The only preset that can touch tickets or the events RFP admin routes.
concierge preset: service_requests.view/assign/update.
kitchen preset: service_requests.view/update (no assign) — useful for demonstrating a 403 on assign.
housekeeping preset: service_requests.view/update (identical to kitchen's).
guest_token is pre-populated with a live token for Ahmad Khalil (checked-in demo guest, phone {{ahmad_phone}}) — just hit Send. Use this for instant guest-side testing without the OTP dance.
No real SMS/WhatsApp/email provider is wired yet — the code is always 000000 in local/testing (see Verify OTP below). Rate-limited to 1/min, 5/hr per identifier.
The code is always 000000 in local/testing — no need to change it. On success this overwrites guest_token (Ahmad) with Rania's — re-run "Ahmad is pre-logged-in" to switch back.

Layla is booked (has_booking=true) but NOT checked in (is_checked_in=false) — use her token ({{layla_guest_token}}, set manually as this request's auth or copy into guest_token) to test tier-3a-only endpoints and the no_active_reservation gate on tier-3b ones.
Links Omar's cancelled demo reservation (booking_code CARL-DEMO0004) via second factor.

item.name: 03 - Public Content
item.description: No token required. Bilingual fields are always {en, ar} objects — Accept-Language only affects the message/error strings.

request.description: Soft-holds a room and sends an OTP. No real provider is wired yet — the code is always 000000 in local/testing, so Step 2 already has it filled in. The phone here (+963900555000) must match Step 2's exactly — the verify step checks the identifier, not just the code.
Uses the reservation_uuid captured from Step 1 automatically. Discards the returned token if simulating the website; keep it if simulating the app.
Layla is confirmed but not checked in — this both assigns the room and flips her reservation to checked_in.

item.name: 06 - Events (RFP)
item.description: Admin routes require the "events" role (tickets.view/assign) — reception/super_admin also work since super_admin bypasses, but {{events_token}} demonstrates the real minimum permission.

item.name: 07 - In-Stay & Pre-Arrival (Guest)
item.description: Uses {{guest_token}} — must be Ahmad (checked_in) for the is_checked_in-gated service-requests endpoints; has_booking-only guests (e.g. Layla) can still book/upload but not place service requests.

request.description: Reads the seeded demo record — safe, read-only.

item.name: [Guest] ⚠️ Approve checkout (destructive — flips reservation to checked_out)

request.description: Only run this if you are OK with Ahmad no longer being checked_in for the rest of your testing session — re-seed to reset.

item.description: cms.edit — only super_admin holds this permission among the seeded accounts, so use {{staff_token}}. The public read shapes (Module 04) mirror these exactly.

item.name: 12 - Staff Management (Admin)
⚠️ Deactivate staff (trainee — safe target, not used elsewhere)

item.description: staff.manage — NO seeded role preset includes this; only super_admin (via bypass) can call this whole folder, so it always needs {{staff_token}}.

item.name: 15 - Chat (Admin)
item.description: tickets.view / tickets.respond — the "events" preset holds both.

item.name: 16 - Operations Queue & Dashboard (Admin)
❌ Assign a service request (as Kitchen — expect 403, no service_requests.assign)
Update ticket status (as Events, resolve it)

item.description: Assign/status permission differs by {type} (service-requests vs tickets) — see the role-specific examples above. GET routes just need service_requests.view|tickets.view, which {{staff_token}} (super_admin) always has.
